category_grades.name: A. Architecture and Boundaries
category_grades.description: Assesses source organization, package boundaries, and separation of first-party concerns.

category_grades.name: B. Build and Dependency Management
category_grades.description: Checks whether build and dependency declarations are explicit and reproducible.

category_grades.name: C. Configuration and Environment Hygiene
category_grades.description: Checks committed environment/tool configuration and local setup clarity.

category_grades.name: D. Contracts, Types, and Domain Modeling
category_grades.description: Evaluates Design by Contract signals: validation, types, assertions, and explicit invariants.

category_grades.name: E. Reliability and Error Handling
category_grades.description: Reviews tests plus explicit validation, exception, and failure-path handling.

category_grades.name: F. Function, Module Size, and SRP
category_grades.description: Evaluates coarse function/module size and single responsibility risk using static size signals.

category_grades.name: G. Testing Discipline and TDD
category_grades.description: Evaluates automated test presence and TDD support; commit history was not used to prove TDD workflow.

category_grades.name: H. CI/CD and Release Safety
category_grades.description: Checks workflow files and release automation gates.

category_grades.name: I. Code Style and Static Analysis
category_grades.description: Looks for formatters, linters, type-checker configuration, and style enforcement.

category_grades.name: J. API Design and Encapsulation
category_grades.description: Evaluates API surface and Law of Demeter risk from organization and oversized modules.

category_grades.name: K. Data Handling and Persistence
category_grades.description: Checks schema, migration, serialization, and persistence evidence.

category_grades.name: L. Observability and Logging
category_grades.description: Checks logging, diagnostics, and operational visibility signals.

category_grades.name: M. Maintainability, DRY, DbC, LoD
category_grades.description: Explicitly evaluates DRY, Design by Contract, Law of Demeter, and maintainability signals.

category_grades.name: N. Scalability and Operational Readiness
category_grades.description: Checks deploy/build readiness and scaling signals from CI, config, and project structure.

key_risks: Split oversized modules to restore SRP and maintainability

prioritized_recommendations.title: Split oversized modules to restore SRP and maintainability
prioritized_recommendations.problem: Oversized first-party files indicate single responsibility and DRY risks.
prioritized_recommendations.impact: Large modules increase review cost, hide duplicated logic, and weaken Law of Demeter boundaries.
prioritized_recommendations.fix: Extract cohesive units behind small interfaces, then pin behavior with tests before refactoring.
acceptance: Largest modules are split by responsibility.
Callers depend on narrow interfaces rather than deep object traversal.

coverage_notes: Reviewed tracked first-party files from git ls-files, excluding cache, build, vendor, virtualenv, and generated output directories.
Reviewed 479 tracked files, including 102 code files, 41 test-like files, 1 CI files, 2 build/dependency files, and 24 documentation files.
This is a read-only static assessment. TDD history and full Law of Demeter semantics cannot be proven without commit-by-commit workflow review and deeper call-graph analysis.
